summary: All 4 core components built. Gateway-side bridge compiles and tests pass. Extension code complete but needs workspace registration for TypeScript compilation.
completed: Chrome Extension Core (Manifest V3) — all source files created
Auth Bridge — WebSocket client in background.ts, content script command execution
Passive Driver Generator — interaction recorder + defineSite() export in content.ts
Gateway WebSocket Endpoint — ExtensionBridge class + /v1/dev/:device/:op route + tests

gateway_modified: packages/std/src/gateway.ts (added WebSocket upgrade, generic device route)

next_steps.action: Register chrome-extension workspace in root package.json
next_steps.details: Ensure 'packages/*' workspace glob includes chrome-extension or add it explicitly
chrome://extensions → Developer mode ON → Load unpacked → select packages/chrome-extension/dist/

details: Open extension popup, click 'Share Tab' on a test page
Verify click executes on the tab and response returns { ok: true, result: { clicked: true } }

extension_to_gateway_flow: 1. Extension background.ts connects WebSocket to ws://localhost:3500/ext
2. Sends ExtHello with clientId + list of shared tabs (deviceId: tab-${tabId})
3. Gateway ExtensionBridge tracks device ownership: deviceId → clientId
http_request_flow: 3. If yes: extBridge.callDevice() sends ExtDeviceRequest via WebSocket
4. Extension background.ts routes to chrome.tabs.sendMessage(tabId, { type: 'bridge_command', op: 'click', payload })
5. Content script executes click, sends response back via sendResponse
6. Background forwards ExtDeviceResponse to gateway via WebSocket
7. Gateway returns HTTP 200 { ok: true, result: { clicked: true } }
passive_recorder_flow: 1. Content script observes clicks, typing, navigation
2. Records events to chrome.storage.local with best-effort selectors
4. Content script builds defineSite() code from recorded events
5. Returns TypeScript code, popup copies to clipboard

testing.gateway_tests: packages/std/src/__tests__/ext-bridge.test.ts — 5 test suites covering WebSocket connection, share updates, request/response, timeout, cleanup
testing.extension_tests: None (manual testing required for Chrome extension UI + runtime)

notes: Extension uses ES2022 module syntax, requires Chrome MV3 service worker support
Password fields are redacted as '<redacted>' in passive recorder
Navigation tracking uses history.pushState/replaceState monkeypatching for SPA support
WebSocket reconnection is automatic with 1.5s backoff
Device ownership is cleared on WebSocket disconnect
Generic /v1/dev/:device/:op route falls back to existing LLM routes for backward compat
